evidence_quotes.quote: spread lies and falsehood to confuse people
consumers were given back control in form of alerts of scheming businesses and sites
if there was something like a cyber watch that warns about these things so the consumer can make a better judgement

paragraphs.text: I was born at a time when there was no internet and as much as people love to think back fondly of those times, they tend to forget how horrendous these times were. It was difficult and time consuming to gain access to good informations, only the most privileged enjoyed quality information. When the internet came out and was the "new kid in the block" and more and more information were one search away, it was so exciting, we were getting closer to equality. Sadly we got to a turning point where nefarious business practices, people with hidden agendas and criminal intent started mudding the water and turned the internet into a tool of which they can spread their scientifically disproven contents and gain influence, start a gambling campaign in form of crypto currencies, non fungible tokens or similar and spread lies and falsehood to confuse people and fill every possible site with advertisements or "news feed". I think we would be going in the right direction if consumers were given back control in form of alerts of scheming businesses and sites, if there was something like a cyber watch that warns about these things so the consumer can make a better judgement. An example would be the indicators from Google and Wikipedia that points out falseness of claims made by deceivers. I also think a push is needed towards archiving lost media for the sake of human history, art and culture. It should be considered a human rights to be able to bask in the richness of timeless literature, music, film, art and game and the destruction of such should be seen as an erasure of a part of human creativity.
